macro_summary: Markets ended Wednesday nearly flat (SPY -0.12%, QQQ -0.14%) as the Iran-Hormuz crisis enters its 17th day with no resolution in sight, per CNBC. VIX fell 6% to 25.33, offering temporary relief but not yet signalling a trend reversal — the S&P 500 remains below its 100-day average. The dominant macro driver remains the Fed's hawkish rate pause: the 10-year Treasury yield slipped to 4.33% today from 4.39% yesterday, a modest easing that slightly reduces duration pressure on tech multiples without changing the higher-for-longer thesis. Per Bloomberg, a US 15-point draft plan to end the Iran war is circulating, which drove gold lower and oil modestly higher on the day. Non-Farm Payrolls on April 3 is the next major scheduled catalyst.
commodities_note: WTI data shows a conflict between sources: the headline figure shows $91.67 (+3.07% daily), but the historical close suggests $92.35 (-0.7%). The fast-info source is used as the headline; the 5-day trend is more reliable — WTI is broadly holding the $88-92 range with a geopolitical premium structural floor from the Hormuz disruption. Gold also has a data conflict: the headline shows $4,512 (-1.22% daily) versus a historical close of $4,399 (+2.6%). Using the fast-info headline of $4,512, gold remains elevated — near all-time highs — driven by geopolitical flight-to-safety demand, but pulled back today on the reported US peace draft per Bloomberg. At $4,512 on confirmed conflict uncertainty, gold remains well above any attractive entry (GLD would be commensurately extended above its 50-day average), so no entry is warranted despite the strong signal. The dollar (DXY +0.31% to 99.59) continues to reflect the US rate premium over ECB and other central banks.

D05_SI.signal_rationale: Signal changed from CAUTION to HOLD — DBS has crossed back above its SGD 57.00 support with RSI at 55.1, and yesterday's CAUTION reflected a brief dip below that level that has now recovered. Price at SGD 57.37 sits fractionally below the 50-day average of SGD 57.50 (0.23% gap), so the technical picture is neutral rather than deteriorating. The risk/reward is 2.7:1 to the nearest resistance at SGD 58.29, with invalidation at the SGD 57.00 support zone — a tight stop of only 0.6% below. Tight stop — normal intraday volatility may trigger invalidation before the thesis is tested; use the structural 200-day average at SGD 52.38 (8.7% below) as the mental stop for position sizing. The NIM compression narrative (US10Y at 4.33%, still well above the 4.20% reset threshold) remains the structural headwind, and the analyst consensus implies -8.8% earnings growth — a meaningful drag on the thesis. The 5.35% dividend yield provides a cushion, but volume is below average, so no entry dislocation exists today. Bottom line: DBS is fairly priced for a bank in a rate-pause environment, but it's not cheap enough to act on. Wait for either a pullback toward SGD 55-56 or a sustained break above SGD 58.29 with volume before considering entry.

risk_reward.invalidation_reason: 50-day SMA -- break below confirms rally failure

O39_SI.signal_rationale: Signal unchanged at WATCH. OCBC is 2.37% above its rising 50-day average of SGD 21.10 with RSI at 59.2 — technically the cleanest setup in the Singapore cluster, and three sessions above the 50-day average with the SMA itself trending higher. However, the risk/reward is unfavourable at 0.4:1: the nearest resistance at SGD 21.81 offers only 1.0% upside against 2.3% downside to the 50-day SMA invalidation level. The PEG ratio of 4.49 is high — OCBC's 12.1x forward PE looks modest in isolation, but analyst consensus projects only 2.7% earnings growth, making the multiple less compelling than it appears. The structural support sits at SGD 18.29 (200-day SMA, 15.3% below) — a long way down if the thesis breaks. Volume remains below average, offering no confirmation of the three-day rally. The Hormuz crisis adds secondary logistics cost pressure as a Singapore airline hub risk for the broader economy. Becomes actionable if price pulls back to the SGD 21.10 50-day average support with volume confirmation, or on a clean break above SGD 21.81 resistance. Bottom line: OCBC is the best Singapore bank on the watchlist right now, but chasing a 2.4% extension above the average into thin resistance is poor risk management — wait for a better entry.

U11_SI.signal_rationale: Signal changed from CAUTION to HOLD — UOB at SGD 36.87 is now 1.46% below its 50-day average of SGD 37.42, and per Simply Wall St., the bank priced a CNY 5 billion fixed-income offering (including Eurobond and Panda bond tranches) and redeemed prior notes, a constructive liability management move that modestly improves the fundamental picture. This is a valid reason to move off CAUTION — the NIM compression headwind persists, but an active debt profile simplification is a positive operational signal. The risk/reward is poor at 0.4:1: only 1.4% upside to the SGD 37.40 resistance versus 3.6% downside to the 200-day SMA at SGD 35.53. Analyst consensus expects -6.7% earnings growth, and the PEG of 2.34 with -1.8% revenue growth makes valuation difficult to justify at any premium. The forward PE of 10.1x is a 27.3% discount to the Singapore cluster median — genuine value, but not yet a catalyst for re-rating without a rate environment shift. Hold with no action; upgrades to WATCH on a close above SGD 37.42 with volume. Bottom line: UOB is cheap but stuck — the debt management move is a good sign, but you need the US rate environment to cooperate before this unlocks.

C6L_SI.signal_rationale: Signal unchanged at CAUTION. SIA at SGD 6.62 sits 0.41% below its 50-day average of SGD 6.65, in the acceptable entry zone technically, but the fundamentals remain deeply problematic. The Hormuz crisis is now in its 17th day, disrupting regional airspace and forcing airline reroutes per Logicall — British Airways, Air France KLM, and Singapore Airlines are among carriers halting services to key Middle Eastern destinations. This directly impairs SIA's route economics and belly-cargo revenue. More critically, analyst consensus expects a 64.3% earnings decline — the most severe on the entire watchlist. Per the earnings decline prominence rule: a 64% expected earnings collapse is incompatible with a WATCH or BUY signal absent clear recovery catalysts within 2 quarters, and none are visible while the Hormuz disruption persists. The 8.28% FCF yield is the one genuine positive. Invalidation would be a close below the 200-day SMA at SGD 6.55 (1.1% below); tight stop — use the SGD 6.34 structural support (4.2% below) as the mental stop. Bottom line: SIA is a value trap while the Middle East conflict continues to close regional airspace — the 64% earnings decline forecast is the single most important reason to stay away.

risk_reward.invalidation_reason: 50-day SMA -- break below confirms rally failure

Z74_SI.signal_rationale: Signal unchanged at WATCH. SingTel at SGD 4.99 is 2.92% above its rising 50-day average of SGD 4.85, and has held above that average for 38 consecutive sessions — a sustained trend that is note-worthy for a defensive name. RSI is neutral at 50.8, well off overbought territory. However, the risk/reward is only 1.1:1 — just 3.2% upside to the SGD 5.15 resistance versus 2.8% downside to the 50-day SMA invalidation level — below the 2.0x threshold required for ACCUMULATE. The valuation concern is real: at 24.7x forward PE with 0.9% revenue growth and a PEG of 3.8, SingTel is 77.7% above the Singapore cluster median PE — a significant premium that requires justification. No specific catalyst is visible near-term to close that gap. The 2.64% dividend provides some support. Structural support sits at SGD 4.38 (200-day SMA, 12.2% below) if the 50-day average breaks. Becomes actionable on a pullback to the SGD 4.85 50-day average with volume, where the R:R improves materially. Bottom line: SingTel's trend is the cleanest in the Singapore cluster, but you'd be paying a high price for slow growth — wait for a pullback to SGD 4.85.

NVDA.signal_rationale: Signal unchanged at CAUTION. Invalidation at $177.41 (0.7% below current price of $178.68) — tight stop: normal intraday volatility may trigger invalidation before the thesis is tested. Structural support at $169.81 (5.0% below) is the more meaningful thesis test; use that level for position sizing. NVDA sits just below the 200-day average of $178.95, 2.94% below its declining 50-day average — the technical picture is bearish, with price trapped in a compression zone between structural support and the declining SMA50. The R:R is 4.6:1 to the nearest resistance at $184.47, which is theoretically favourable, but the CAUTION signal reflects the base_scorer's judgment that the SMA50 is declining and price is below it. ACCUMULATE gate check: all mechanical gates pass for NVDA — caution_source is base_scorer, R:R 4.6:1, rr_quality observed, VIX < 30, no earnings within 5 days (May 21). Checking for ticker-level thesis break: none identified. Jensen Huang's claim that AGI has been 'achieved' is a bold bullish assertion from a seller — treat with appropriate skepticism. Reuters reports Reflection AI is raising $2.5 billion at a $25 billion valuation, backed by NVDA — an indirect validation of AI infrastructure demand. The Blackwell Ultra ramp remains on track per NVDA's own Q4 guidance. ACCUMULATE — starter position. Entry zone $177-179 (near 200-day SMA support), invalidation at $177.41 (use $169.81 structural support as mental stop for sizing). R:R 4.6:1 to $184.47 resistance. PAPER TRADE — under evaluation. This is the first ACCUMULATE signal for NVDA this week. Bottom line: NVDA is reasonably priced for its growth rate despite the choppy price action — the thesis is intact and the structural support is right here. A starter position at current levels makes sense; the 200-day average is the line in the sand.

risk_reward.invalidation_reason: 50-day SMA -- break below confirms rally failure

AMD.signal_rationale: Signal changed from HOLD to WATCH — AMD surged 6.44% today on reports that AMD and Intel told clients CPU price increases are coming, per Yahoo Finance, with AI data center demand cited as the driver. The 5-day gain of 10.4% triggers the pipeline's momentum chase block (>10% 5-day move), preventing a BUY signal despite AMD's fundamentals being among the strongest on the watchlist: 34.1% revenue growth, 217% expected earnings growth, PEG of 0.6, and the confirmed Meta GPU supply deal (up to 6GW of AMD Instinct GPUs through 2030, per AMD IR press release). Volume is high and confirms the move. Invalidation at the 50-day SMA of $214.70 (2.5% below); structural support at $194.36 (200-day SMA, 11.8% below). R:R is only 1.0:1 at current levels — just 2.6% upside to the $225.98 nearest resistance against 2.5% downside. The pipeline block is the right call: chasing a 10%+ week is poor risk management regardless of the thesis. Catalyst override does not apply — price is only 2.59% above SMA50 but the 5-day block is independent of the SMA distance rule. INTC earnings on April 24 could provide a read-through. Bottom line: AMD is the cheapest quality name on the watchlist relative to its growth rate — but let the dust settle after a 10% week. Wait for a pullback toward $214-216 before entering.

INTC.entry_block: CAUTION: forward P/E of 48x (>40x). Requires explicit justification of why earnings growth supports this multiple — default to WATCH unless thesis is clearly articulated with specific catalysts and timeline.

risk_reward.invalidation_reason: 50-day SMA -- break below confirms rally failure

INTC.signal_rationale: Signal changed from CAUTION to WATCH — Intel surged 6.21% today alongside AMD on CPU price increase reports, breaking above its 50-day average of $46.63 for the first time (now 1.19% above, at $47.18). The pipeline flags a soft block for the 47.6x forward PE, requiring explicit justification: Intel's 18A foundry roadmap under the CHIPS Act, combined with the tariff-driven supply chain onshoring thesis, justifies a tentative upgrade from CAUTION. The price increase reports directly support near-term revenue recovery. Invalidation at the 50-day SMA of $46.63 (1.2% below) — tight stop: normal intraday volatility may trigger invalidation. Use the structural support at $42.58 (9.7% below) as the mental stop for position sizing. R:R is 3.5:1 to the nearest resistance at $49.17 — favourable. However, FCF yield is -1.91% (negative — Intel is burning cash), revenue growth is -4.1%, and no earnings growth estimate is available from analysts. Volume is normal, not high. The 47.6x forward PE is 132% above the semis cluster median — extreme premium for a company with negative FCF and revenue decline, even with foundry optionality. INTC earnings April 24 (29 days out) will be the thesis validator. Entry strictly at or below the 50-day average ($46.63) with a tight stop. Bottom line: Intel is a turnaround story that just got a price catalyst — it's worth watching, but the negative cash flow and premium valuation mean you need the April 24 earnings to confirm the recovery before sizing up.

ASML.signal_rationale: Signal changed from WATCH to HOLD — ASML fell 1.73% today, slipping 0.06% below its 50-day average of $1,394.77, which flips the signal. The confirmed €7.9 billion SK Hynix EUV order — the largest publicly disclosed equipment contract in ASML's history, per Simply Wall St. — is a genuinely bullish fundamental development that validates the 2030 revenue target. However, the R:R at current levels is effectively 0.0:1: only 1.0% upside to the nearest resistance at $1,407.54 against 25.8% downside to the 200-day SMA. That asymmetry makes entry unjustifiable regardless of the fundamental quality. The High-NA EUV ramp with Intel 14A (H2 2026, per ASML earnings call) is the medium-term catalyst. Forward PE of 31.9x with 4.9% revenue growth and PEG of 2.2 — expensive relative to current growth, though the long-cycle nature of semiconductor equipment makes trailing metrics less relevant than backlog. ASML earnings April 15 (20 days out) is the next signal-change candidate. No action until that print clarifies guidance. Bottom line: ASML is a world-class business with a landmark order, but the price is right at its average and the reward-to-risk is almost zero at this exact level — patience before the April 15 earnings will be rewarded.

MU.signal_rationale: Signal unchanged at CAUTION. Micron fell 4.76% today (5-day decline of 17.25%) after StockStory reports Google announced something that could reduce demand for memory chips — a material ticker-level development worth flagging even without full details. This contradicts the HBM datacenter demand thesis that supported WATCH in recent sessions. Price at $382.09 is 5.4% below the declining 50-day average of $403.88, firmly in CAUTION territory. The valuation data requires scrutiny: the 3.9x forward PE and 0.26 PEG look anomalously low — with 196.3% revenue growth and 756% expected earnings growth, these figures appear to reflect an extraordinary earnings inflection from the memory cycle trough. FCF yield is only 0.67% despite the apparent low PE, and the 16.0% dividend yield is almost certainly a data anomaly (likely reflecting a special distribution or data error — verify before acting). R:R is 3.0:1 to $437.83 resistance with invalidation at $363.90 (4.8% below). The structural support at $357.67 (6.4% below) is the meaningful thesis test. The Google demand-reduction announcement is a specific ticker-level development that warrants maintaining CAUTION rather than upgrading, despite the otherwise attractive valuation setup. ACCUMULATE gate check: caution_source is base_scorer and R:R ≥ 2.0, but the Google announcement constitutes a specific ticker-level thesis risk — withholding ACCUMULATE. Bottom line: Micron's memory cycle recovery thesis is real, but today's Google news introduces demand uncertainty that you don't want to fight — wait for clarity before adding.

AMZN.signal_rationale: Signal unchanged at CAUTION. Amazon gained 1.34% today as the company announced its acquisition of Fauna Robotics to expand in the humanoid robot market, per Motley Fool — a long-term strategic move, not a near-term earnings catalyst. Price at $211.71 sits 3.17% below the declining 50-day average of $218.65. The risk/reward shows an 18.0:1 ratio, which sounds exceptional, but the tight invalidation at $211.22 (only 0.2% below) makes this misleading — the stop is nearly at current price. Tight stop: normal intraday volatility may trigger invalidation before the thesis is tested. Use the structural support at $196.93 (7.0% below) as the mental stop for position sizing. ACCUMULATE gate check: caution_source is base_scorer, R:R 18.0:1, rr_quality observed, VIX < 30, no earnings within 5 days. However, the rr_quality for the 18.0:1 calculation relies on a stop that is 0.2% below current price — functionally unusable as a real stop. The structural support-based R:R (to $219.43 from $196.93) is the operative framework, which is approximately 3.5:1 — still above 2.0x, but the tight invalidation issue warrants maintaining CAUTION rather than ACCUMULATE. FCF yield of 1.05% is low. The $200B AI capex commitment (per AMZN 10-K) sent shares down 10% and remains unvalidated by earnings. PEG of 1.92 is reasonable but not compelling. Bottom line: Amazon has a lot going for it strategically, but the stock is stuck below its declining average and the short-term setup doesn't have a clean entry point — wait for a move back above $218 with volume.

GOOG.signal_rationale: Signal unchanged at CAUTION. Alphabet fell a further 0.79% today after Tuesday's larger decline; a landmark US court ruling found Meta and Google liable for $6 million in damages over addictive platform design — $1.8 million against Google — per Reuters. The $1.8 million is financially trivial, but the ruling's precedent is not: plaintiff lawyers described it as 'the tip of the spear' with thousands of similar cases pending, and Meta/Google have said they will appeal. This adds a structural legal risk overhang that is new and meaningful. Price at $289.59 is 8.26% below the declining 50-day average of $315.67 — deep in CAUTION. RSI at 33.0 is approaching oversold territory with above-average volume today, suggesting distribution is ongoing. The R:R is 0.1:1 — 0.7% upside to the nearest resistance at $291.53 against 9.5% downside to the 200-day SMA at $261.97. That asymmetry rules out any entry. The 200-day SMA at $261.97 is the structural support (17.9% below current price) and the level where CAUTION would be re-evaluated. GOOG earnings April 24 (29 days out) is the next potential signal-change catalyst. PEG of 2.24 reflects expensive-for-growth territory. FCF yield is only 1.09%. Bottom line: Google is getting cheap on a PE basis, but falling knives need to slow down before you reach for them — the legal precedent set today adds a new layer of uncertainty, and the stock needs to find support near $260-262 before the setup improves.

LITE.signal_rationale: Signal unchanged at CAUTION. Lumentum fell 5.22% today — a notable pullback after being added to the S&P 500 on March 23, 2026, per 24/7 Wall St. — consistent with the learned heuristic that S&P 500 inclusion buying typically exhausts within 3-5 sessions. Price at $777.17 remains 37.85% above the 50-day average of $563.78, triggering the extreme extension hard block with no exceptions. The NVDA $2 billion strategic investment (confirmed via NVIDIA investor relations press release, March 2, 2026) validates the AI optics thesis for the 2026-2028 period. The PEG of 0.15 appears anomalous — with a 51.9x forward PE and 65.5% revenue growth, the implied earnings growth estimate would need to be extraordinary; flag as 'PEG appears anomalous — verify earnings growth estimate' and do not cite it as a primary argument. FCF yield is essentially zero (-0.04%). The sma50_warning applies: the 50-day SMA is 27% below price — do not suggest a pullback to SMA50 as an entry. The nearest usable support is $573.73 (the upper support zone), still 26.2% below. No entry exists at any level above $650 given the extreme extension. Bottom line: Lumentum's AI optics story is real and confirmed by NVDA's investment, but after a near-1000% one-year run, there's no sensible entry point — let the dust settle from the inclusion rally before revisiting.

COHR.signal_rationale: Signal unchanged at HOLD. Coherent pulled back 3.36% today after a strong run, sitting 16.05% above its 50-day average of $234.42 — blocked from entry by the >5% extension hard block, with RSI at 57.8 above the 55 threshold that would allow a trend exception. The NVDA $2 billion strategic investment in silicon photonics (confirmed via NVIDIA press release, March 2, 2026) and S&P 500 addition on March 9, 2026 remain the thesis anchors. Today's pullback is modest relative to the extension — from $272.04, reaching the $234.42 50-day average would require a 13.8% decline. The PEG of 0.06 is almost certainly anomalous (do not cite as primary argument — verify earnings growth estimate against the 36.6x forward PE). At 36.6x forward PE, COHR trades at a 17.2% discount to the photonics cluster median — the only valuation positive. R:R is 0.8:1 — unfavourable. The $230.15 support zone (15.4% below) is the structural level that would represent a genuinely interesting entry point for a thesis-intact pullback. Bottom line: Coherent's fundamentals are supported by NVDA's investment, but the stock is too extended to enter responsibly — wait for a pullback to the $230-235 zone before acting.

XLE.signal_rationale: Signal unchanged at CAUTION. XLE at $60.57 is 12.08% above its 50-day average with RSI at 77.5 — deeply overbought, blocked by both the extension hard block and the extreme RSI. The Hormuz crisis (17 days in, 2 vessels/day versus 138 baseline) provides the structural supply premium, but energy equities at this extension level create a profit-taking vulnerability that overrides the geopolitical fundamental. Per CNBC, Bloomberg reports a US 15-point peace plan is circulating — if confirmed, a resolution could see oil prices fall sharply from current elevated levels, removing the premium that has driven XLE 10% higher in the past month. The nearest resistance is at $63.60 (5.0% upside); the derived downside to the 50-day SMA is 10.8%. No entry exists at current levels. If WTI reverses toward $75-80 on a Hormuz resolution, XLE would likely correct sharply — the geopolitical driver has not graduated to structural (it has been active less than 60 days and has not caused a fundamental re-rating of energy sector earnings beyond the near-term commodity price). Bottom line: XLE is overbought on a geopolitical premium that could evaporate quickly on peace deal news — avoid entry at these levels and watch for a 10-12% pullback if the Hormuz situation de-escalates.

PLTR.signal_rationale: Signal unchanged at WATCH. Palantir at $154.96 is 3.1% above its 50-day average of $150.31, with RSI at 54.0 — modestly extended but not blocked. The pipeline flags the 83x forward PE as a soft block requiring justification. The justification exists and is specific: the $10 billion Army IDIQ framework ceiling (indefinite delivery/indefinite quantity — not firm revenue) and the confirmed $448 million Navy ShipOS deal (per DoD press release) provide concrete defense demand validation. The 2026 revenue guide of $7.18-7.20 billion (+61% YoY) is ambitious but tied to visible government contracts. Per Barron's, analysts note the Golden Dome missile defense project could generate billions for PLTR — though Rosenblatt's $200 price target raise is an analyst projection, not confirmed revenue. The R:R is 6.9:1 to $187.08 resistance from the $150.31 invalidation — highly favourable if the thesis holds. FCF yield of 0.34% is very low, and the 267% premium to the BigTech cluster median PE is significant. The invalidation is clean: a break below the $150.31 50-day SMA confirms rally failure. Structural support at $148.06 (4.5% below). PLTR earnings May 5 (40 days out). Bottom line: Palantir is expensive by any conventional metric, but defense AI spending is genuinely accelerating, and the government contract visibility justifies a premium — watch for a pullback to $150-152 for a cleaner risk/reward entry.

VUAA_L.signal_rationale: Signal unchanged at CAUTION. Would reconsider above $130.24 resistance with volume confirmation and VIX sustained below 22.

singapore.summary: The cluster continues its 16th consecutive session under structural pressure — NIM compression from the Fed's 3.50%-3.75% rate pause and US10Y at 4.33% (above the 4.20% reset threshold) keeps the rerating catalyst out of reach. Notable divergence today: DBS upgrades to HOLD on support recovery while UOB moves to HOLD on proactive debt restructuring, but neither is actionable for a fresh entry. SIA remains the cluster's weakest fundamental link with the 64% earnings decline forecast compounded by Hormuz airspace disruptions; OCBC remains the only name approaching actionability.
singapore.key_development: UOB's CNY 5 billion Eurobond/Panda bond offering and concurrent note redemption represents the first positive liability management signal from the Singapore cluster in weeks.

semiconductors.summary: SOXX at $345.25 is essentially flat (-0.01%) versus yesterday's $341.02 close — a muted session masking significant individual divergence. AMD and INTC both surged 6%+ on CPU price increase reports, while MU fell 4.76% on Google demand-reduction news and ASML dipped 1.73% back below its 50-day average. The cluster is bifurcating: names with near-term pricing power catalysts (AMD, INTC) outperformed, while memory (MU) faces a new demand headwind and equipment (ASML) consolidates ahead of April 15 earnings.
semiconductors.thesis_status: Bifurcated — pricing catalyst stocks vs. memory/equipment consolidation
semiconductors.key_development: Google's announcement of a potential memory chip demand reduction drove MU's 4.76% decline, directly challenging the HBM datacenter thesis that had supported WATCH status.

big_tech.summary: The cluster remains uniformly on CAUTION. AMZN gained 1.34% on robotics news but remains below its declining 50-day average; GOOG fell further on the social media liability verdict, adding a precedent-setting legal risk to existing technical weakness; PLTR holds WATCH status as the one differentiated name with defense AI contract visibility insulating it from commercial capex cycle headwinds.
big_tech.thesis_status: Under pressure — duration headwinds and new legal overhang for GOOG
big_tech.key_development: A US court found Google liable for $1.8 million in damages in the first-ever social media addiction trial, per Reuters — with thousands of similar cases pending, setting a legal precedent that is more significant than the dollar amount.

photonics.summary: Both names pulled back today — LITE -5.22% and COHR -3.36% — consistent with S&P 500 inclusion buying exhausting within the expected 3-5 session window. The NVDA investment thesis remains intact for both (confirmed via NVIDIA press releases), but extreme extension in LITE (38% above SMA50) and meaningful extension in COHR (16% above SMA50) keep both blocked from entry. The divergence worth noting: COHR's pullback is bringing it marginally closer to actionable territory.
photonics.thesis_status: Thesis intact, technicals extended — no entry
photonics.key_development: Both LITE and COHR pulled back today as S&P 500 inclusion momentum fades, with LITE now 5.22% off Tuesday's all-time high — early signs of the post-inclusion consolidation phase.

energy.summary: XLE ended virtually flat (+0.03%) as WTI data shows conflicting signals around the $91-92 range. The Hormuz crisis structural premium persists at day 17, but Bloomberg's report of a US 15-point peace draft introduces the first credible de-escalation signal — a resolution would sharply deflate the geopolitical premium currently embedded in XLE at RSI 77.5.
energy.thesis_status: Overbought on geopolitical premium — de-escalation risk rising
energy.key_development: Per Bloomberg, the US has drafted a 15-point plan to end the Iran war — if confirmed, this is the first material downside risk to the Hormuz premium that has driven XLE 10% higher over the past month.

index.summary: VUAA.L at $127.18 (RSI 39.3) remains on CAUTION, 3.27% below its declining 50-day average with VIX at 25.33. The index tracker is approaching oversold territory but has not yet generated a reversal signal. Until VIX drops sustainably below 22 and SPY reclaims its 100-day average, the defensive posture is appropriate.
index.key_development: VIX fell 6% today to 25.33, the largest single-day decline in the past week — a necessary but not sufficient condition for a trend reversal in broad equity exposure.

interconnected.name: Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Co. (ADR)
interconnected.reason: Primary fab for AMD's MI450 GPU (Meta $100B+ deal confirmed via AMD IR), NVDA Blackwell Ultra ramp, and INTC's third-party chip orders — any acceleration or delay in these programs flows directly through TSMC's CoWoS (chip-on-wafer-on-substrate, advanced packaging) capacity utilisation.
interconnected.entry_note: Add to watchlist if TSM pulls back 8-10% from current levels, approaching its 50-day average — the AMD Meta deal ramp starting H2 2026 is the timing anchor.

interconnected.name: Broadcom
interconnected.reason: Custom AI ASIC supplier to Google (TPU v7) and Meta — Google's TPU v7 expansion for Anthropic and Apple (per Google Cloud Next 2025 keynote) directly drives Broadcom networking and custom chip revenue, making it a direct read-through for GOOG's AI infrastructure spending.
interconnected.entry_note: Revisit after GOOG April 24 earnings for confirmation that TPU v7 deployment pace is accelerating — a positive GOOG cloud revenue print would strengthen the AVGO thesis.

interconnected.name: SK Hynix
interconnected.reason: Just signed a record €7.9 billion multi-year EUV equipment order with ASML (confirmed via Simply Wall St. citing the contract) — SK Hynix is the primary HBM memory supplier to NVDA's Blackwell platform and directly links ASML's order book to NVDA's AI chip production ramp.
interconnected.entry_note: Becomes interesting for the watchlist if the KRW price (approximately $85-95 USD equivalent per share via ADR) pulls back 10% from current levels on broader emerging market weakness — the NVDA Blackwell ramp is the multi-quarter demand anchor.

active_risks: Iran-Hormuz crisis day 17: Strait traffic at 2 vessels/day vs. 138 baseline; WTI holding $88-92 range with structural supply premium; Kuwait and Iraq output cuts compound disruption.
US-China semiconductor tech war: Section 301 investigation ongoing; China countering with export controls on critical minerals and US entity list designations; tariff escalation risk into 2026.
US tariff turmoil: Supreme Court struck down IEEPA tariffs; administration pivoted to Section 122 (15% surcharges, 150 days); China retaliated with 34% tariffs and export controls.
Social media liability legal precedent: US court found Meta and Google liable in first youth addiction trial; thousands of similar cases pending, creating open-ended litigation exposure for BigTech.
geopolitical.market_impact: The Hormuz crisis keeps XLE deeply overbought (RSI 77.5, blocked from entry) while simultaneously pressuring SIA's (C6L_SI) route economics with the 64% earnings decline forecast reflecting direct conflict exposure. The Fed's hawkish pause at 3.50%-3.75% continues compressing GOOG and AMZN below their declining 50-day averages, while the new social media liability verdict adds a non-trivial legal cost overhang to GOOG specifically.
geopolitical.new_since_yesterday: Per Bloomberg, the US has drafted a 15-point plan to end the Iran war — the first concrete de-escalation framework since the crisis began, driving gold lower and oil marginally higher as markets weigh credibility. Additionally, a US court delivered the first-ever social media addiction liability verdict against Meta and Google (per Reuters), setting legal precedent for thousands of pending cases.
geopolitical.portfolio_action: Hold cash positions and avoid new entries into overbought energy or extended tech; selectively accumulate NVDA near 200-day SMA support and watch AMD and INTC for post-momentum-block pullback entries.

events_this_week.event: GOOG social media liability verdict fallout — appeal process begins; monitor for institutional repositioning
Non-Farm Payrolls (March) — strong jobs print would reinforce higher-for-longer thesis, pressuring tech multiples; weak print would raise rate cut probability and could trigger broad rally
ASML Q1 2026 earnings — could resolve HOLD signal; EUV order book update and High-NA adoption pace will determine whether WATCH signal is warranted
INTC Q1 2026 earnings — thesis validator for 18A foundry ramp and CPU pricing power; confirms or denies the WATCH upgrade from today
GOOG Q1 2026 earnings — key test of cloud growth sustainability and initial legal cost provisions from social media liability verdict
AMD Q1 2026 earnings — first major update on Meta GPU supply deal ramp progress and CPU pricing realization; critical for validating WATCH signal upgrade
AMD Instinct MI450 first shipments to Meta (1GW tranche) — confirms GPU supply deal execution and revenue recognition ramp per AMD IR press release

portfolio_snapshot.risk_posture: The Fed's hawkish rate pause (US10Y 4.33%, pause at 3.50-3.75%) remains the dominant constraint — it keeps 7 tickers on CAUTION and prevents multiple expansion across tech, semis, and Singapore banking simultaneously.

accumulate.note: ACCUMULATE — starter position. Blackwell Ultra ramp thesis intact; sitting on 200-day SMA support with tight but defined invalidation. PAPER TRADE — under evaluation.
accumulate.entry_level: Enter $177-179 (near 200-day SMA $178.95), invalidation at $177.41; use $169.81 structural support as mental stop for sizing. R:R 4.6:1 to $184.47.

watch_for_entry.note: Blocked by 10%+ 5-day momentum chase rule despite strong fundamentals (PEG 0.6, Meta GPU deal confirmed). Actionable on pullback — do not chase.
watch_for_entry.entry_level: Enter $214-216 (50-day SMA reclaim), stop below $194.36 (200-day SMA). R:R improves materially at that level.

watch_for_entry.note: Just broke above 50-day SMA on CPU pricing news; tight invalidation and negative FCF make this a watch-and-confirm setup ahead of April 24 earnings.
watch_for_entry.entry_level: Enter at or below $46.63 (50-day SMA), stop below $42.58. Wait for earnings confirmation before sizing up.

watch_for_entry.note: Defense AI contracts validate 83x forward PE partially; pullback to 50-day SMA would improve risk/reward to near 7:1. Not a chase above $155.
watch_for_entry.entry_level: Enter $150-152 (50-day SMA support), stop below $148.06. R:R 6.9:1 to $187 resistance.

watch_for_entry.entry_level: Enter at SGD 21.10 (50-day SMA), stop below SGD 20.01 (100-day SMA). Current price too extended for entry.

on_deck.note: Memory cycle recovery thesis intact on blowout Q2 2026 earnings, but Google demand-reduction news today introduces near-term uncertainty. Technically blocked 5.4% below declining 50-day SMA.
on_deck.rationale: 3.9x forward PE (anomalous — verify), 196% revenue growth; even with data anomaly caveats, the memory cycle inflection is real. FCF yield low (0.67%) but earnings trajectory is exceptional.
on_deck.trigger: Stabilise above $363.90 support with volume for 2+ sessions, AND Google demand concern clarified with no follow-through from other hyperscalers cancelling HBM orders.

caution_trim.note: Avoid. Stock 8.3% below declining 50-day average, social media liability verdict adds new legal cost overhang, and PEG of 2.24 is expensive for current growth. R:R 0.1:1 — no entry exists.
Avoid. Tight invalidation 0.2% below current price makes the 18:1 R:R ratio misleading; use structural stop at $196.93. FCF yield 1.05% and $200B capex unvalidated by earnings.
Avoid. 37.9% above 50-day SMA — hard-blocked at extreme extension. S&P inclusion buying appears to be exhausting after today's 5.2% pullback. No entry until sub-$650.
Avoid. RSI 77.5, 12% above 50-day SMA, and Bloomberg-reported US peace draft introduces de-escalation risk to the Hormuz premium driving this rally.
Avoid. Analyst consensus expects 64% earnings decline; Hormuz airspace disruptions directly impair SIA route economics with no near-term resolution catalyst.
Avoid. Broad S&P 500 exposure below declining 50-day average with VIX at 25.33; wait for sustained VIX compression below 22.

hold_no_action.note: Upgrades to WATCH on sustained close above SGD 58.29 with volume. Reassess below SGD 57.00 support (CAUTION re-entry).
Upgrades to WATCH on close above SGD 37.42 50-day average with volume. CNY bond restructuring is positive but insufficient alone.
Upgrades to actionable WATCH if pullback reaches SGD 4.85 50-day SMA. 24.7x PE with 0.9% revenue growth — no rush to enter at premium.
No action until April 15 earnings. €7.9B SK Hynix order is bullish context; upgrades to WATCH if earnings beat with upward guidance revision.
Upgrades to WATCH if pullback reaches $234-230 support zone. NVDA investment thesis intact but 16% extension above 50-day SMA keeps entry blocked.
